definition: A projected 1.0-1.5 Mt copper deficit by 2027-28 is the most binding physical constraint on AI capex execution. Mine pipeline adds ~2-3 Mt through 2030 but grade decline removes ~1-1.5 Mt effective supply while AI + electrification demand adds 3-4 Mt. Discovery-to-production timelines of 16+ years are structural.
evidence: Global production ~22-23 Mt with identified project pipeline data
Development timelines of 16+ years not price-responsive
LME copper stocks at 150-180 kt vs 350 kt 5-year average confirm current tightness
Pair_1 debate: commodity analysis stronger on actionable near-term risk identification
Demand projections carry ±30% uncertainty and recycling/substitution could close 10-20% of gap
open_questions: Can recycling and substitution close more than 20% of the gap?
Does geographic bifurcation of AI capex deployment alter the constraint?
What copper price would trigger sufficient demand destruction to close the gap?
